visual_checks: Look for any content that flashes, blinks, or rapidly alternates in brightness or color
Check for animated elements, GIFs, videos, or canvas animations that may contain flashing
Verify that no content flashes more than three times per second regardless of size or area
Look for strobe effects, rapid transitions, or pulsing animations
Check video content for scenes with rapid light changes (explosions, lightning, camera flashes)
Look for blinking text, flashing cursors, or rapidly toggling UI indicators
pass_conditions: No content on the page flashes more than three times in any one-second period
All animations, videos, and dynamic content remain below the three-flash threshold with no exceptions
Flashing content, if any, is limited to fewer than three flashes per second regardless of the size of the flashing area
No strobe effects, rapid brightness changes, or rapid color alternations exceed three flashes per second
fail_conditions: Any content flashes more than three times in any one-second period, regardless of size or brightness
Animated GIFs, videos, or canvas elements contain sequences exceeding three flashes per second
Strobe or pulsing effects in any element flash more than three times per second
Rapid alternation between contrasting colors or brightness levels exceeds three times per second
Video content includes scenes with flashing that exceeds three per second (even briefly)
na_conditions: Page contains no flashing, blinking, or rapidly changing content
All content is static with no animations, videos, or dynamic visual changes
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Animated or video content could not be loaded to evaluate flash rates
common_mistakes: Assuming the general flash threshold from SC 2.3.1 (small area or below thresholds) applies — this AAA criterion has no size or threshold exception
Animated GIFs with rapid frame changes that produce flashing above three per second
Video content with brief strobe-like scenes (e.g., concert footage, action sequences) that exceed the limit
CSS animations with rapid opacity or color transitions that create flash effects
Loading spinners or progress indicators that flash or pulse more than three times per second
Ignoring user-generated content (embedded videos, uploaded GIFs) that may contain excessive flashing

examples.pass: All animations on the page use smooth transitions and no element changes brightness or color more than three times in any one-second period; video content has been reviewed and contains no flashing sequences
examples.fail: A promotional banner uses a strobe animation effect that alternates between bright white and dark blue five times per second to draw attention to a sale
examples.partial: Most page content is flash-free, but an embedded video contains a brief 2-second scene with camera flashes exceeding three per second

plain_meaning: AAA version of 2.3.1: NOTHING may flash more than three times per second, regardless of area or luminance thresholds. The intent is absolute safety for photosensitive users.
scope_applies_to: Any flashing content exceeding 3 flashes per second, at any size
Video, GIFs, CSS/JS animations, embedded media
borderline_calibration: Any flash rate above 3/second fails, regardless of flash area.
If 2.3.1 passes because flashes are below area threshold but the rate is above 3/second, this SC (AAA) still fails.
